title: Using unencrypted SNS topics is security-sensitive
description: Using unencrypted SNS topics is security-sensitiveAnalyze your coderesponsibility-trustworthysecuritySecurity HotspotawscweAmazon Simple Notification Service (SNS) is a managed messaging service for application-to-application (A2A) and application-to-person (A2P) communication. SNS topics allows publisher systems to fanout messages to a large number of subscriber systems. Amazon SNS allows to encrypt messages when they are received.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Resources:
  Topic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: my-topic  # Sensitive - no encryption

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Resources:
  Topic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: my-topic
      KmsMasterKeyId: alias/aws/sns  # Sensitive - uses default key without explicit policy
compliant:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Resources:
  Topic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: my-topic
      KmsMasterKeyId: arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012
      DisplayName: Encrypted Topic

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Resources:
  TopicPolicy:
    Type: AWS::SNS::TopicPolicy
    Properties:
      Topics:
        - !Ref Topic
      PolicyText:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal: '*'
            Action: 'kms:Decrypt'
            Resource: '*'

checks.description: Detects unencrypted SNS topics that lack proper KMS encryption configuration
